---
title: "One Tool to *Rule Them All*"
subtitle: "![](assets/logo-quarto.svg){width=1in}for Documentation, Presentations, Manuscripts and Outreach"
number-sections: false
---

# Welcome

Quarto is an open-source scientific and technical publishing system created by Posit. It helps researchers move from scattered workflows to a more integrated way of writing, documenting, presenting, and sharing their work.

This workshop website is designed as a guided reference. You can follow it from top to bottom during a live session, or return to individual pages later when you need help with a specific output format.

::: {.callout-note}
## Workshop idea

The core promise of Quarto is simple: write once in a plain text source file and render to multiple professional outputs such as HTML, PDF, DOCX, and slides.
:::

## Who We Are

::: {.team-grid}

::: {.quarto-grid-item .card-team}
::: {.team-image-container}
![](assets/Ben.jpg){.team-image .lightbox}
:::

[Ben Black]{.team-member-name}

[Head of the group Data and Modelling Infrastructure for Living Labs]{.team-job-title}

[ETH Zürich]{.team-employer}

::: {.team-member-links}
[{{< iconify fa6-solid house-chimney-user size=xl >}}](https://blenback.github.io/){.listing-link-icon}
[{{< iconify fa6-solid envelope size=xl >}}](mailto:bblack@ethz.ch){.listing-link-icon}
[{{< iconify fa6-brands linkedin size=xl >}}](https://www.linkedin.com/in/ben-black-9889a1150/){.listing-link-icon}
[{{< iconify fa6-brands github size=xl >}}](https://github.com/blenback/){.listing-link-icon}
[{{< iconify fa6-brands researchgate size=xl >}}](https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Benjamin-Black-5/){.listing-link-icon}
:::
:::

::: {.quarto-grid-item .card-team}
::: {.team-image-container}
![](assets/isabel.jpg){.team-image .lightbox}
:::

[Isabel Nicholson-Thomas]{.team-member-name}

[Doctoral Researcher]{.team-job-title}

[ETH Zürich]{.team-employer}

::: {.team-member-links}
[{{< iconify fa6-solid house-chimney-user size=xl >}}](https://irl.ethz.ch/people/person-detail.Mjg5Mjgx.TGlzdC8zNzc5LC0xMzk1OTgzMDM3.html){.listing-link-icon}
[{{< iconify fa6-solid envelope size=xl >}}](mailto:nharisena@ethz.ch){.listing-link-icon}
[{{< iconify fa6-brands linkedin size=xl >}}](https://www.linkedin.com/in/nivedita-varma-harisena-667298141/){.listing-link-icon}
[{{< iconify fa6-brands github size=xl >}}](https://github.com/NVHarisena1){.listing-link-icon}
:::
:::

::: {.quarto-grid-item .card-team}
::: {.team-image-container}
![](assets/manuel.png){.team-image .lightbox}
:::

[Manuel Kurmann]{.team-member-name}

[Research Assistant]{.team-job-title}

[ETH Zürich]{.team-employer}

::: {.team-member-links}
[{{< iconify fa6-solid house-chimney-user size=xl >}}](https://pedroribalmeida.com.br/){.listing-link-icon}
[{{< iconify fa6-solid envelope size=xl >}}](mailto:pedroribalmeida@gmail.com){.listing-link-icon}
[{{< iconify fa6-brands linkedin size=xl >}}](https://www.linkedin.com/in/perial/){.listing-link-icon}
[{{< iconify fa6-brands github size=xl >}}](https://github.com/Zapiano){.listing-link-icon}
:::
:::

:::

## Who This Workshop Is For

This material is especially useful for:

- early career researchers building their first durable workflow
- experienced researchers who want fewer disconnected tools
- teams that care about reproducibility, transparency, and reuse
- anyone who wants websites, manuscripts, presentations, and notes to feel part of the same system

Basic familiarity with at least one language such as R, Python, or Julia can help, but it is not required for most of the lessons here.

## What You Will Learn

By working through this site, participants will learn how to:

- understand what Quarto is and where it fits relative to R Markdown
- write basic content using Markdown and YAML
- install Quarto and understand the main tooling options
- use Quarto for lab notebooks, manuscripts, presentations, websites, and dashboards
- explore more advanced tools such as extensions, templates, and `brand.yml`

## How To Use This Site

The workshop is organized in three parts:

1. Start with the Foundations pages if Quarto is new to you.
2. Move into the Academic Use Cases to see how one tool supports different research outputs.
3. Finish with the Advanced Topics when you are ready to customize or scale your work.

Each use-case lesson has a separate guided exercise page. The lesson explains the workflow, and the exercise helps you practice it with an academic scenario.

## Why Quarto Matters For Researchers

Academic work usually produces many related outputs:

- rough notes and exploratory documentation
- figures and tables for papers
- slides for talks
- websites for projects, labs, or personal profiles
- interactive communication for stakeholders or collaborators

Quarto makes these outputs easier to manage because the underlying authoring model stays consistent. That means less time relearning tools and more time focusing on the research itself.

::: {.callout-tip}
## Suggested path

If you are brand new, begin with [What Is Quarto?](foundations/quarto-intro.qmd) and then continue to [Markdown for Beginners](foundations/markdown-intro.qmd).
:::
